theory_reading: The current theory is no longer mainly about extensional completeness through ordinary support-style interfaces. It is becoming a structural theory of residual witnesses, exact recognition interfaces, and one-sided conservativity, with the frontier now centered on finite residual control and internal recognition machinery rather than orientation-blind support claims. The main question is how to turn already exact residual interfaces into durable structural criteria that look like metatheorem-shaping boundaries rather than a pile of local equivalences.
keep: Structural correspondences between language hierarchies and logical systems
Extraction of logical rules and complexity
Closeness to established notation and theory

focus_now.label: finite_residual_control
focus_now.thesis: Treat finite residual control as the main structural invariant that could reorganize the current theory.
focus_now.why_now: The theory snapshot says exact residual and graph interfaces already exist, while desired summary changes emphasize subformula-bounded root and run control and a finite reachable region.
valued_problem_types: Extraction of logical rules and complexity
target_result_shapes: subformula bounds for residual root states
finite reachable-region theorems for accepting residual runs
criteria turning residual exactness into finite-search control

focus_now.label: internal_recognition_interfaces
focus_now.thesis: Refine the agenda toward self-contained internal recognition or witness frameworks rather than external graph packaging.
focus_now.why_now: Both the theory snapshot and the missing-bridges list point to reformulating residual recognition as a closed internal branching automaton and to replacing external graph dependence with intrinsic witnesses.
target_result_shapes: branching automaton style internal recognition theorems
closed recognition formalisms replacing external graph machinery

focus_now.label: sharp_handed_boundaries
focus_now.thesis: Use handed and residual criteria to formulate exact boundaries that replace broad support-style statements.
focus_now.why_now: Verified counterexamples rule out ordinary SupportClosure exactness, flat atom-occurrence containment, counting bounds, and same-handed ordinary-support exactness, so the productive direction is now sharp boundary criteria rather than generic support claims.
target_result_shapes: criteria distinguishing residual acceptance from weaker support notions
boundary theorems explaining why orientation-blind support fails

deemphasize_now.pattern: orientation-blind support or counting claims stated without exact residual or handed interfaces
deemphasize_now.reason: The theory state already records verified failures and overexploration in this direction, so such claims are unlikely to rewrite the top-level summary.

deemphasize_now.pattern: local witness refactorings that do not add finite-search control or internal automata structure
deemphasize_now.reason: These may clean up existing exactness statements but do not advance the theory toward durable structural criteria.

deemphasize_now.pattern: singleton, atom-only, or fragment corollaries already dominated by arbitrary-context or sequent-level exactness
deemphasize_now.reason: The overexplored-pattern list indicates that these are already structurally subsumed and no longer agenda-defining.

boundary_evidence: Ordinary SupportClosure exactness is not the right global frame; future agenda should be phrased through residual or handed criteria instead.
Flat atom-occurrence and counting-style bounds are not reliable structural summaries of derivability in the current setting.
Same-handed support phenomena need exact thresholds or converse failures, not broader support reformulations.
agenda_guardrails: Keep the working agenda at the level of theorem families and structural interfaces, not concrete next lemmas.
Prefer themes that would still matter if several nearby proof attempts fail.
Use counterexamples to sharpen the framing of the theory, not to accumulate isolated negative statements.
Do not let tactical next-step guidance replace the higher-level reading of what theory is being built.
